case_title: OMNICARE CONSULTING MEDICAL GROUP, INC. VS. BSML, INC., AKA BRITE SMILE, A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al

EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE AND TRO, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF OMNICARE CONSULTING MEDICAL GROUP, INC.
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE DEFENDANTS RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION HEARING SET FOR APR-14-2010 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER
POS OF OSC; EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR OSC AND TRO; VERIFIED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF OMNICARE CONSULTING MEDICAL GROUP, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANT IS ENJOINED FROM ISSUING CHECKS IN PLAINTIFF'S NAME, EMPLOYING PEOPLE IN PLAINTIFF'S NAME OR UNDER PLAINTIFF'S IDENTIFICATION NUMBER, AND MAKING ANY USE OF PLAINTIFF'S NAME. NO BOND REQUIRED BECAUSE DEFENDANT WAIVED ITS RIGHTS UNDER CCP SECTION 529. SEE SMITH VS. ADVENTIST HEALTH SYSTEM WEST, (2010) 182 CAL.APP.4TH 729. ORDER RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-14-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF AUG-27-2010 IS OFF CALENDAR DISMISSAL ON FILE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-17-2010 Off Calendar

Order To Show Cause Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Order to Show Cause re: preliminary injunction. Appearances: Mitchell J. Green, Esq. of Curtis & Green (415) 456-4600 appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Order to Show Cause re: preliminary injunction is granted. No opposition filed. Defendant is enjoined from issuing checks in plaintiff's name, employing people in plaintiff's name or under plaintiff's identification number, and making any use of plaintiff's name. No bond required because defendant waived its rights under CCP section 529. See Smith vs. Adventist Health System West, (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 729. Order re: preliminary injunction signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
